calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($980,716 USD / 46,687 total beneficiaries) = $21.01 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 48 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 4%, Companion Animals: 96%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: 38,817 TOTAL NO OF ANIMALS TREATED

metrics.context_qualifier: Anti-Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in Bangalore, part of the ABC-ARV initiative.

source.quote: 11372 TOTAL ARV DONE

metrics.context_qualifier: Animal Birth Control program for stray dogs in Bangalore, set up and monitored by BBMP.

source.quote: 5792 TOTAL ABC DONE

metrics.context_qualifier: Organized truckloads of hay, grass, fodder, and grains for large working animals and strays during COVID-19 lockdown.

source.quote: 2000+ TOTAL NUMBER OF ANIMALS FED

metrics.context_qualifier: Provided care for sick and injured stray animals, including dogs, cats, and large animals, in Bangalore.

source.quote: 1944 TOTAL ANIMALS TREATED

metrics.context_qualifier: Subsidized cat sterilization program (Trap & Neuter Release) for community cats in Bangalore.

source.quote: 606 TOTAL NUMBER OF CATS STERILISED AND VACCINATED

metrics.context_qualifier: Investigated and followed up on cases of intentional or inadvertent cruelty to animals in Bangalore City.

source.quote: 425 TOTAL CRUELTY CASES ATTENDED, PURSUE OFFICIALS, FILE OFFICIAL COMPLAINTS AND FOLLOW UP.

metrics.context_qualifier: Rehomed dogs from the Second Chance Adoption Centre into new, safe, and appropriate homes.

source.quote: 264 TOTAL ANIMALS ADOPTED

metrics.context_qualifier: Total cats neutered since the inception of the Trap Neuter Release program.

source.quote: Since the program's inception, CUPA has successfully neutered over 3000 cats with the support from Help Animals India, the HCL Foundation, Robert Bosch Engineering & Business Solutions Pvt Ltd and cat lovers from across the city.
A total of 5000+ animals were fed on a daily basis.

significant_events.summary: Provided uninterrupted supply of feed to thousands of stray and shelter animals (dogs and large animals) affected by lockdown.

significant_events.event_name: Inauguration of New Small Animal Specialty Hospital

source.quote: On 14 January, 2022 the CUPA board along with the management inaugurated the beautifully designed hospital to kick off a new and modern era of veterinary operations under CUPA's wing.

significant_events.summary: Inaugurated a new 5000 sq ft state-of-the-art hospital providing comprehensive veterinary care for pets at subsidized rates.

significant_events.event_name: Animal Birth Control (ABC) & Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV) Program

source.quote: The ABC-ARV program is set up and monitored by BBMP and has been in place for many years.

significant_events.summary: Ongoing program for stray dogs in Bangalore, involving sterilization and vaccination, monitored by BBMP.

source.quote: Ajay Arjun, CUPA's head of projects and resources spearheaded the cat sterilisation project, scaling up cat sterilisation efforts for the organisation in different different parts of the city through structured Trap & Neuter programmes with volunteers from local residential associations.

significant_events.summary: Scaled up cat sterilization efforts through structured Trap & Neuter programs with volunteers in different parts of Bangalore.

source.quote: We were successful in taking custody of these dogs through a landmark decision of the Karnataka High Court.

significant_events.summary: Successfully took custody of dogs from illegal breeders through a landmark Karnataka High Court decision and worked with the State Animal Welfare Board for registration and licensing.

source.quote: The Trauma Centre is at the forefront of CUPA's promise to provide care for sick and injured stray animals throughout the city.

significant_events.summary: Provides emergency care for sick and injured stray animals, including dogs, cats, and large animals, across Bangalore.

source.quote: Second Chance Adoption Centre (SCAC) is located on a 2-acre property in Dommasandra, providing plenty of space for dogs to be socialised and rehabilitated.

significant_events.summary: Provides shelter, socialization, and rehabilitation for abandoned or abused dogs, preparing them for adoption.

significant_events.event_name: Mylappanahalli Geriatric Centre Relocation and New Dwellings

source.quote: The construction of the new dwellings was completed despite state lockdowns and strict protocols, just in time for the summer of 2021.

significant_events.summary: Relocated and completed construction of new dwellings for over 60 elderly, blind, and handicapped senior dogs.

source.quote: 425 TOTAL CRUELTY CASES ATTENDED, PURSUE OFFICIALS, FILE OFFICIAL COMPLAINTS AND FOLLOW UP.

significant_events.summary: Attended to 425 cruelty cases, pursuing officials and filing complaints to address intentional or inadvertent harm to animals.

significant_events.event_name: Large Animal Rescue & Rehabilitation Centre (LARRC) Operations

source.quote: This centre was founded in 2012 with the objective of rescuing and rehabilitating large working animals.

significant_events.summary: Rescues and rehabilitates large working animals like cows, bulls, buffaloes, donkeys, horses, and goats that have outlived their economic value or are injured.

significant_events.event_name: Small Animal Specialty Hospital Grooming Section Makeover

source.quote: The CUPA grooming section also got a complete makeover.

significant_events.summary: Upgraded the grooming section with new equipment and automatic bathtubs to provide stress-free grooming for pets.

source.quote: AMAZING STORIES - RESCUES AND TREATMENT

significant_events.summary: Specific cases of rescue and treatment, including a mother and foal, a discarded husky, a dog with a large tumor, and a cat with severe mite infestation.

source.quote: Thoroughbreds that are no longer fit for racing generate no profit for their owners, and instead of receiving the strict care that these sensitive and fearlessly loyal animals require, they are either silently euthanized or thrown out onto the road.
The Mylappanahalli Geriatric Centre suffered a major setback at the start of 2020 and were asked to vacate abruptly.

data.original_text: CUPA mainly takes care of animals that are hurt, sick or abandoned and gives them care and treatment until they recover, after which they are re-homed or rehabilitated.

The Karnataka-based organisation has a network of 7 centres, each dedicated to a critical aspect of animal well-being, Animal Birth Control, Trauma & Rescue Centre, Second Chance Adoption Centre, Geriatric Centre, and Large Animal Rehabilitation Rescue Centre, accommodating around 300 animals in total.
CUPA also engages in ventures such as pet cruelty inspections and advocacy for animal rights.
CUPA has also recently initiated the T.N.R. program for cats.
It also helps in the rehabilitation and housing of wildlife in their indigenous forest zones.
CUPA is also involved with legal issues protecting the interest and welfare of animals, wild and domestic, in the High Court of Karnataka and the Supreme Court.
Empowers to rescue, provide relief, and prevent cruelty to animals through medical care, adoption, and sterilization.
One such initiative involves sponsoring meals at the CUPA Large Animal Rescue & Rehabilitation Centre, providing nourishment for large working animals like cows, bulls, and goats.
Another opportunity is to sponsor meals at the CUPA Geriatric Centre, which houses over 60+ blind and handicapped senior dogs in need of ongoing care.
Additionally, contributions to the trauma and rescue centre can support free medical, surgical, and post-operative care for injured, sick, or abandoned animals.

The CUPA Small Animal Specialty Hospital is a modern facility offering comprehensive veterinary care for pets. With a skilled team of veterinarians, it provides a range of services from cosmetic procedures to medical surgeries. The hospital also offers housing and post-operative care facilities. Its services include vet consultations, grooming, sterilization, vaccinations, diagnostic imaging (X-ray, ultrasound, ECG), blood tests, surgeries, trauma care, and a pet shop with a pharmacy.
This well-established animal welfare organisation runs seven centres in Bangalore, including a veterinary hospital and a geriatric centre.

They have a Corporate Social Responsibility programme under which you can help with donations, sponsorships or passive adoption of their shelter animals, employee volunteering activities or group visits at their shelters, along with hosting awareness programmes, fundraisers or adoption drives for employees or on corporate campuses.
CUPA is actively involved with the rescue and rehabilitation of ill, injured and abandoned street animals in Bangalore.
CUPA is a 29-year-old organisation that has undertaken a number of diverse avatars in order to rescue and rehabilitate injured and sick animals across Bangalore City.
The organisation has carried out tireless work towards animal welfare over the decades.
This is the first and largest community-driven sterilization program in Bangalore.
From two tiny rooms in her house, CUPA today operates from seven centres in the city of Bangalore.
More than 10,000 wild and domestic animals pass through its portals
Over the years, it has treated a significant number of animals, totalling 398,121.

Compassion Unlimited Plus Action has impacted, 1,200,122 animals positively affected, including 4,861 adoptions, 166,689 dogs sterilized, 344,681 dogs vaccinated, 3,041 cats sterilized, 3,042 cruelty cases attended, 149,606 emergency trauma cases, 86,489 large animals treated.
The CUPA Trauma & Rescue Centre... has treated a significant number of animals, with a total of 149,606 cases attended to.
Additionally, the centre has facilitated 329 adoptions, finding loving homes for animals in need.
